content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
File: 
Section 8l009(b) 
Date 
June 26, 1986 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
:lary Ann Kvasager 
Subject: Preservation of Reports and Statements 
Request for advice from the City Clerk of Delano 
FACTS: 
The City of Delano held an election in 1984. 
In 1986 
the city clerk ~isposed of the original and copies of all 
campaign disclosure statements filed by the unsuccessful candi-
dates involved in the 1984 election. 
The clerk has since 
received a request from the public sector to see copies of the 
disclosure statements filed by all of the candidates involved 
in this election. 
One solution the clerk came up with was to ask the candidates 
to reconstruct their ca'11paign statements if they could not 
supply her with a copy. 
It was decided at the opinion request meeting of June 26 that 
the clerk should not request the candidates to reconstruct 
their statements. 
She should write to each of the unsuccessful 
candidates requesting a copy of their disclosure statements 
filed in connection with this election. 
The clerk is aware of 
the fact that some of the candidates do not have copies of their 
campaign statements. 
In this instance, the clerk might want to 
contact the local newspaper or the incumbent candidate involved 
in this election to see if they have copies. 


TO: 
) J. LARSON 
( ) R. BROvffi 
FROM: Kvasager 
) G. BAUGHER 
( ) A. HERNDON 
) B. LEIDIGH 
( ) J. PRITCHARD 
DATE: 6/29/86

(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS) NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

NO [ ] 
QUESTION: 
In 1986, the city clerk disposed of the originals and copies 
of ca~paign statements filed by unsuccessful candidates involved in 
a 1984 city election. Section 81009(b) requires that original statements 
of candidates not elected be retained by filing officers for a period 
of not less than five years. 
The clerk asked if she could reguire the 
candidates to reconstruct their statements when a copy could not be supplied. 
ANSWER: 
The clerk should not request the candidates to reconstruct 
their statements. 
She shOUld write to each of the unsuccessful 
candidates requesting a copy of their dislcosure statements. 
Sjnce some 
of the candidates did not retain copies, the clerk might want to 
contact the local newspaper or the incumbent candidate 
jnyo]yed jn 
this election to see if they have copies.
